segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: Yes, there's all sorts of possibilities.
segments.zh: 是的，有各种各样的可能性。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: And somehow motivate consumers to change, yes.
segments.zh: 并且以某种方式激励消费者去改变，是的。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.zh: 嗯。他们可能在那里冒很大的风险。

segments.speaker: MARIE
segments.en: They might be taking a big risk there.

segments.speaker: COLIN
segments.en: Yes, it's hard to know which varieties are safe to eat.
segments.zh: 是的，很难知道哪些品种是安全可食用的。不管怎样，也许现在……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For my presentation today, I'm going to talk about the Céide Fields in the northwest of Ireland, one of the largest Neolithic sites in the world.
segments.zh:
在我今天的演讲中，我将介绍爱尔兰西北部的Céide Fields，这是世界上最大的新石器时代遗址之一。我最近参观了该遗址，并观察了目前由一队考古学家在那里进行的工作。
该遗址最初是在20世纪30年代由一位当地教师Patrick Caulfield发现的。他注意到当地人在沼泽中挖掘时，不断碰到似乎是成排的石头。他意识到这些一定是墙壁，而且它们一定有几千年历史，因为它们在后来覆盖其上的沼泽形成之前就已经存在了。
他写信给都柏林的国家博物馆，请他们进行调查，但没有人认真对待他。直到40年后，Patrick Caulfield的儿子Seamus（当时已成为考古学家）开始进一步探索。他将铁探针插入沼泽中，以绘制石头的形成图，这是当地人几千年来一直用于寻找埋在沼泽中的燃料的传统方法。碳定年法后来证明该遗址有5000多年的历史，是爱尔兰最大的新石器时代遗址。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I recently visited this site and observed the work that is currently being done by a team of archaeologists there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The site was first discovered in the 1930s by a local teacher, Patrick Caulfield.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He noticed that when local people were digging in the bog, they were constantly hitting against what seemed to be rows of stones.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He realised that these must be walls and that they must be thousands of years old for them to predate the bog which subsequently grew over them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He wrote to the National Museum in Dublin to ask them to investigate, but no one took him seriously.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It wasn't until 40 years later, when Patrick Caulfield's son Seamus, who had become an archaeologist by then, began to explore further.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He inserted iron probes into the bog to map the formation of the stones, a traditional method which local people had always used for finding fuel buried in the bog for thousands of years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Carbon dating later proved that the site was over 5,000 years old and was the largest Neolithic site in Ireland.

items.prompt: an important Neolithic archaeological site in the northwest of Ireland

pairs.p: one of the largest Neolithic sites
pairs.note: 'important' 被替换为 'largest'，强调规模大，但都是正面描述
完全相同的短语，直接定位
题干中的 'site' 在原文中具体化为 'the Céide Fields'，这是该遗址的名称

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的 'northwest of Ireland' 直接定位到原文第一句，然后发现 'Neolithic site' 对应 'largest Neolithic sites'，而答案 'walls' 出现在第二段，由 'rows of stones' 推断为 'walls'。

items.prompt: His ... became an archaeologist and undertook an investigation of the site:

pairs.note: His 指代前文提到的 Patrick Caulfield，原文用所有格形式明确关系。
原文与题干几乎一致，只是时态略有不同。

pairs.q: undertook an investigation of the site
pairs.note: undertook an investigation 同义替换为 began to explore further，表示开始进一步探索。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 Patrick Caulfield's son Seamus，其中 son 对应题干 His，而 Seamus 就是题干中的人，后面描述他成为考古学家并开始探索，与题干完全匹配。

items.prompt: a traditional method used by local people to dig for ... was used to identify where stones were located

pairs.q: a traditional method used by local people to dig for ...
pairs.p: a traditional method which local people had always used for finding fuel
pairs.note: 原文中'finding fuel'对应题干'dig for ...'，fuel是答案。

pairs.p: map the formation of the stones
pairs.note: 'map the formation'等同于'identify where ... were located'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'He inserted iron probes ... to map the formation of the stones, a traditional method which local people had always used for finding fuel'，其中'finding fuel'直接对应题干'dig for ...'，因此答案为fuel。

items.prompt: Items are well preserved in the bog because of a lack of ...
Houses were ... in shape and had a hole in the roof.
pots used for storage and to make ...
Each field at Céide was large enough to support a big ...
The fields were probably used to restrict the grazing of animals – no evidence of structures to house them during ...
